data.title: Analyst Sees Buy Opportunity in Floor & Decor After Guidance Cut, Citing Structural Advantages and Margin Upside
data.description:
William Blair analyst Phillip Blee has maintained their bullish stance on FND stock, giving a Buy rating on April 29.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Phillip Blee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Floor & Decor’s long-term positioning despite near-term headwinds. He acknowledges that the company cut its full-year sales and earnings outlook after a soft first quarter and ongoing housing-market weakness, but views the resulting share-price pressure as an attractive entry point for investors with a longer time horizon.

He also highlights several structural positives that support future upside, including the company’s ability to take price with limited pushback, an enhanced pricing organization and tools, and competitive advantages versus pressured independents and less engaged big-box rivals. In addition, he points to rising gross-margin expectations as evidence that the business can improve profitability even in a challenging volume and macro environment, which he believes is not fully reflected in current guidance or the stock price.

In another report released on April 29, Telsey Advisory also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $80.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: BMO’s Ari Klein Reiterates Outperform on Ryman, Lifts Price Target to $111 on Strong Q1 Beat and Upgraded 2026 RevPAR Outlook
data.description:
In a report released today, Ari Klein from BMO Capital reiterated a Buy rating on Ryman, with a price target of $111.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ari Klein has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Ryman’s strong operational momentum and conservative guidance framework. The company delivered a robust first quarter, with revenue and profitability metrics exceeding both Street and BMO expectations, supported by solid RevPAR and total RevPAR growth and better‑than‑expected performance at several key properties.

Management also increased its 2026 RevPAR growth outlook, and Klein views the assumptions, particularly around total revenue per available room and out‑of‑room spending, as deliberately cautious. Despite temporary, weather‑driven softness in certain business lines and higher group attrition, he highlights record pricing on new group bookings and a favorable multi‑year growth runway, which together underpin his Outperform rating and the upward revision of the price target to $111.

In another report released on April 22, Wells Fargo also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $114.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on CubeSmart as Solid Operations Support Unchanged $43 Price Target
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Juan C. Sanabria maintained a Buy rating on Cubesmart today and set a price target of $43.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Cubesmart’s solid operating performance and favorable market positioning. The company delivered first quarter results that matched earnings expectations while outperforming on same-store net operating income, supported by stronger revenue trends and improving in-place rental rates, particularly in its New York portfolio, which continued to post robust growth.

Sanabria also underscores that full-year 2026 guidance, though slightly below consensus, still points to an acceleration in earnings in the second half of the year, with disciplined expense control and manageable interest costs. Limited investment activity, modest share count reduction, and steady management fee income contribute to a stable outlook, justifying the unchanged $43 price target and reinforcing his positive stance on the stock’s risk‑reward profile.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
